name: Artichoke Extract
summary: Decision-ready summary: Artichoke Extract is best framed for indigestion support; cholesterol context; bile-flow support; safety screen: bile duct obstruction; gallstones; ragweed allergy.
description: Artichoke Extract supports digestion; cholesterol; bile flow with moderate confidence in this merged decision layer.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v8.2: A randomized placebo-controlled trial found HDL-cholesterol improvement in primary mild hypercholesterolemia.
claimMap.notes: v8.2: A randomized placebo-controlled trial found HDL-cholesterol improvement in primary mild hypercholesterolemia.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: artichoke leaf extract doses vary; 250–2700 mg/day has been used in human studies

sources.title: PubMed PMID 22746542. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 22746542. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: bile duct obstruction due to increased bile flow that can worsen blockage and raise the risk of pancreatitis or cholangitis
gallstones warrants clinician guidance due to a strong choleretic bile-stimulating effect
known allergy to ragweed or other Asteraceae family plants
medications broken down by liver enzymes warrant monitoring since artichoke may alter their clearance rate
pregnancy or breastfeeding due to insufficient safety data
